strengths: ~20 years of continuous software engineering experience across web, mobile, embedded, and infrastructure — exceeds the 15-year minimum
Active involvement in AI-agent infrastructure and MCP framework development at re:cinq demonstrates current, hands-on engagement with agentic AI tooling
Entrepreneurial track record as founder of GISCollective and early-stage contributor at WatchUp, with public speaking at FOSS4G and community workshops showing some thought leadership
concerns: No advanced degree in an AI-related field and no demonstrated AI products or platforms at scale; AI work is recent (April 2025) and limited to internal tooling with no measurable outcomes cited
No evidence of advising strategic customers or partners at an executive or enterprise level — the role requires customer-facing strategic advisory capacity not present in the résumé

Title trajectory (Senior → Staff SWE) and scope of impact remain firmly in individual execution rather than director-level IC influence; no evidence of shaping org-wide or cross-company technical strategy
response.justification: The role's two highest-weight competencies are recognized AI expertise with demonstrated products and the ability to advise strategic customers on cutting-edge cloud and AI adoption. The candidate's AI work is limited to a single recent role entry mentioning MCP tooling with no shipped products, metrics, or external recognition — directly contradicting the preferred qualifier of 'demonstrated products or platforms built with AI.' The customer advisory competency is entirely absent; community workshops and open-source talks do not substitute for executive-level partnership engagements. The candidate does meet the minimum experience bar (~20 years) and shows genuine entrepreneurial instincts via GISCollective, but these positives do not offset the critical gaps in the two competencies that define the role's differentiated value.
